body_txt: Thieves steal for many different reasons; some steal because they are poor and need the money. Others steal because they are greedy . Still, others steal for the thrill and challenge. In addition, who a thief targets can also vary, with some thieves following a strict moral code such as not stealing from the poor or only stealing from evil people while others are indiscriminate in who they steal from. Some particularly heinous thieves will steal from people they dislike solely to hurt them rather than because they desire the object they steal. While thieves overall can vary in their morality, a sadistic thief is almost always portrayed as rotten to the core, as their prime motive is essentially hurting people. Their reasons for hurting people are never sympathetic, with some common ones being envy at the good fortune of others , hatred towards particular individuals, or a desire to see someone miserable . If this type of thief steals money or something of great monetary value, the money is generally either a secondary desire or a bonus to seeing someone be miserable, with some thieves even taking joy in potentially putting their victims(s) in financial hardship. If the thing stolen is something of sentimental value, then the thief won't care if the thing they steal isn't worth much, only that the victim was made miserable by its loss. While some of these types of characters might steal physical items, others might "steal" in more abstract or metaphorical ways. A common example is a homewrecker who steals someone's lover because they want to see someone suffer, but isn't actually interested in the person they steal. These types of homewreckers are quick to throw away the people they steal, though even if they decide to date or marry them long term, they won't hesitate to gloat to the person they cucked. Sub-Trope of Sadist . A Serial Homewrecker could be considered this if they enjoy stealing other people's partners. Overlaps with Seduction as One-Upmanship if someone does this to someone's lover.
